behavior_summary: CLRLAVA::gotHit reacts to a hit on a lava/clear-lava object by producing splash/steam visual effects, playing a splash SFX, and then applying consequences to the attacker (destroy, damage, move off tile, or put avatar in stasis).
The handler guards against re-entrancy/duplicate handling using a global flag 'butthead' and can early-abort behavior if a global 'neverSinks' is set.
Visual/effect placement splits depending on the item's frame value (Item::getFrame compared to 0x20) — two distinct sprite placement/loop patterns are used.

When the attacker is an avatar/NPC the code either destroys the attacker directly or puts them in stasis, recenters the camera, possibly clears death/immortal flags for NPCs, and applies a receiveHit to deal damage; asynchronous cleanup is scheduled via a spawned FREE routine.
notable_control_flow: Early-return if global butthead is set (prevents re-entrancy) or if neverSinks global is set (skips sinking/destruction behavior).
Effect branch based on Item::getFrame() <= 0x20 vs > 0x20 to choose different sprite spawn loops and offsets.
Conditional path based on the third parameter (BP+0Ah) determining whether to destroy the attacker immediately or to put the avatar/NPC in stasis and move them.
NPC-specific path: check/set dead flags, clear immortal/withstand flags, query HP and call Item::receiveHit; camera recenters on affected PID in some branches.
Spawns an asynchronous helper (FREE::1C19) for delayed/cleanup work; another helper (FREE::1A96) is invoked synchronously in one branch.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of the tile/frame threshold 0x20 — inferred to represent two visual/behavioral tile-states but exact tile types are unknown.
Exact interpretation of the third parameter (BP+0Ah) in all contexts — it appears to switch between immediate destruction vs. stasis/move, but caller semantics are not fully certain.
Whether attacked process is always the avatar or may be generic NPCs — code handles both but caller origin is not explicit.
Detailed behavior and responsibilities of FREE::1A96 and FREE::1C19 (cleanup vs. continuation vs. timing) beyond their call sites.
Whether Camera::setCenterOn is always invoked when an NPC/avatar is killed or only under specific sub-conditions.

main_function.name: U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle an incoming hit on a lava-like tile: spawn splash/steam sprites and SFX, and apply the appropriate consequence to the attacker (destroy, move off tile, place avatar in stasis, trigger NPC death/damage) while using guards to avoid re-entrancy and scheduling cleanup.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the lava object receives a U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit event.
Receives parameters: attacker/process id, damage amount, and a third flag/byte that alters the attacker-handling branch.
Runs only when global guards allow it (butthead not set, and neverSinks not preventing sinking behavior).

function_map.name: U8P_CLRLAVA::gotHit
function_map.role: Primary event handler for hits on the lava/clear-lava item; spawns effects and resolves attacker consequences.

function_map.name: U8P_CLRLAVA::FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Synchronous helper/cleanup routine called in the receiveHit branch (appears to prepare/cleanup state before applying damage).

function_map.name: U8P_CLRLAVA::FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Asynchronous helper spawned with a timeout (0x1E) for delayed cleanup/continuation after the immediate reaction.

internal_calls.purpose: Synchronous helper invoked during the branch that applies a receiveHit — likely prepares state/cleans up before damage application.
Spawned asynchronously to handle delayed cleanup or follow-up actions after the immediate hit reaction.

external_calls.purpose: Query the lava item's frame/tile to decide which visual/effect branch to execute (frame <= 0x20 vs > 0x20).
Read the item's X coordinate to position spawned sprites and to compute a target move location for the attacker.
Read the item's Y coordinate to position spawned sprites and to compute a target move location for the attacker.
Read the item's Z/elevation used for sprite placement and branch decisions.
Play a splash/impact sound when the lava tile is hit and sprites are spawned.
Spawn multiple splash/steam sprites around the lava tile with offsets computed from item coordinates; two looping patterns are used depending on frame.
Compute direction from attacker to the lava item; used as the direction argument to Item::receiveHit.
Apply damage/hit to the attacker or NPC (passes direction, damage amount, and an extra uword).
Reset kernel references/refs for attacker or other PIDs when scheduling movement or cleanup; used multiple times to manage process lifetimes.
Immediately destroy the attacker/item in branches that indicate direct removal (third param or other branch conditions).
Put the avatar into stasis (temporary immobilization) instead of destroying them outright in some branches.
Move the attacker/avatar to a safe tile computed from the lava tile coordinates to remove them from the lava position.
Trigger an NPC animation (impact or death) when an NPC attacker is affected.
Check whether the NPC is already dead to decide whether to clear/set dead flags and how to apply receiveHit.
Mark an NPC as dead in the branch that kills the NPC as a consequence of the lava hit.
Center camera on the affected attacker/NPC when a kill/major effect is applied to emphasize the event.
Clear the NPC dead flag when restoring or preparing the NPC for a receiveHit that should affect them normally.
Clear NPC immortality so that receiveHit can actually kill or damage the NPC.
Clear any 'withstand death' flags so the applied hit is effective.
Query NPC HP to compute a lethal or appropriate damage value passed to Item::receiveHit in some branches.

reads.name: butthead (global 0x022D)
reads.likely_effect: Used as an early guard to avoid re-entrancy or overlapping reactions when processing a hit on the lava tile.

reads.name: neverSinks (global 0x000E)
reads.likely_effect: If set, skip full sinking/destruction behavior for the lava tile (prevents attacker-killing behavior).

writes.name: butthead (global 0x022D)
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 while processing the avatar/NPC reaction (stasis/move) and cleared at the end to re-enable normal handling; acts as a temporary processing guard.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: CLRLAVA::gotHit reacts to a hit on a lava/clear-lava object by producing splash/steam visual effects, playing a splash SFX, and then applying consequences to the attacker (destroy, damage, move off tile, or put avatar in stasis).
